message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Tropicale (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~2,899 reviews; Tripadvisor 1,491 reviews (4.3/5, ranked #22 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants); OpenTable shows very high booking volume cited as 'Palm Springs most booked restaurant 2023' [UNVERIFIED exact OpenTable review count]
- Sentiment: mostly positive but polarized — strong on atmosphere/cocktails, mixed on food execution
- Longevity: Opened 2008 (some sources say 2007); ~17-18 years in operation; founding partners Tony Di Lembo, Ranz Weinert, Rob Kurokawa; Di Lembo remains the public-facing chef/owner
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life Best of the Best 2025 (Best Happy Hour)","OpenTable Diners' Choice","OpenTable '100 Hottest Restaurants in America 2021'","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Self-cited 'Palm Springs Most Booked Restaurant 2023' on OpenTable"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Tropicale Palm Springs' direct-name queries and on OpenTable/Tripadvisor downtown Palm Springs lists. Does NOT surface in top-result generative answers for 'best Palm Springs supper club / live jazz' (The Purple Room dominates), 'most romantic Palm Springs' (Le Vallauris, Mister Parker's, Copley's, FARM dominate), or 'best downtown Palm Springs dinner' (Clandestino, 4 Saints, Bar Cecil, Eight4Nine, FARM dominate). Notably absent from major editorial roundups (Infatuation, Bon Traveler, Time Out, Cozymeal) in generative summaries.
- Site structured data: Weak. The Tropicale's site has NO schema.org/JSON-LD Restaurant markup, no Open Graph tags, and no machine-parseable menu/hours structure. Address, phone, and hours appear as plain HTML only. Site is multilingual (7 languages) and has dedicated menu sub-pages (Happy Hour, Brunch, Lunch, Cocktails, Wine List) but none are marked up for agent consumption. A separate apps.thetropicale.com subdomain exists.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Tropicale Palm Springs and Tony Di Lembo:

**Founding and Ownership:**
The Tropicale Restaurant was introduced to Palm Springs in 2007, though according to the restaurant's website, the Tropicale Restaurant was opened in 2008. There's a slight discrepancy between sources regarding whether it was 2007 or 2008.

**Partners:**
The Tropicale was conceived by a trio of partners: Ranz Weinert, Tony Di Lembo, and Rob Kurokawa.

**Tony Di Lembo's Role:**
Tony Di Lembo is the founder Chef and Chef/Owner of The Tropicale Restaurant. Additionally, Tony Di Lembo was once Barbra Streisand's personal chef before opening The Tropicale.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Tropicale in Palm Springs on Tripadvisor:

The Tropicale is rated 4.3 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #26 of 309 restaurants in Palm Springs, with 1,490 unbiased reviews. (Note: The exact ranking and number of reviews vary slightly across different Tripadvisor pages, likely due to continuous updates, but the restaurant consistently ranks in the top 25-26 positions among Palm Springs restaurants.)

The restaurant is highly regarded for its "distinctive world cuisine" with Pacific Rim specialties, as well as its vibrant atmosphere, excellent service, and extensive bar offerings. Reviewers frequently praise the food quality, ambiance, and live entertainment that the restaurant offers.

I found some great information about romantic restaurants in Palm Springs for a date night in 2026. Here are some of the top recommendations:

## Popular Romantic Restaurants

According to Yelp's 2026 top romantic restaurants in Palm Springs include: Clandestino, The Front Porch, Spencer's Restaurant, FARM, Miro's Restaurant, Copley's, Eight4Nine, The Tropicale, Bar Cecil, and Ash & Vine.

## Specific Restaurant Highlights

**FARM**: FARM is a beloved French-inspired eatery offering farm-to-table dishes in a charming countryside setting with a cozy patio adorned with lush greenery and floral arrangements. The menu features traditional Provencal cuisine with popular items like savory crepes, duck confit, and wagyu top sirloin steak.

**Copley's**: Copley's is an award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine infused with hints of tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate. Guests can experience upscale dining either outside on the patio with stunning mountain views or indoors.

**Le Vallauris**: Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a local favorite tucked against the San Jacinto Mountains with thoughtful detail and an intimate setting, making you feel like you've stepped into a charming French home.

**Melvyn's at The Ingleside Inn**: Melvyn's is known for white cloth dining and is one of the last restaurants in Greater Palm Springs to offer tableside cooking with favorites like Steak Diane. After dinner, you can visit the piano bar and lounge for drinks and dancing.

These options offer a variety of atmospheres and cuisines, from French to contemporary American, all perfect for creating a memorable date night experience in Palm Springs.

Based on my search results, I found relevant information about your query:

The Tropicale in Palm Springs received the "Best Happy Hour" award in Palm Springs Life's "Best of the Best 2025". 

The Tropicale is located at 330 E Amado Road, Palm Springs, and their happy hour is offered daily from 4-7 P.M, as well as 4 until close on Wednesday nights.

The Tropicale is where old-school Palm Springs meets South Beach color and a tiki bar twist, and it's loud, lively, and unapologetically flamboyant. Their happy hour menu features 20+ options including Thai chicken skewers with peanut sauce, Mediterranean hummus, tabbouleh and pita, Angus sliders, and a selection of pizzas.

I've completed a web search for the best downtown Palm Springs restaurants for dinner in 2026. Here are some of the top recommendations:

## Popular Downtown Palm Springs Dinner Restaurants

Top-rated options include The Front Porch, Clandestino, Rooftop 262 Bar & Grill, Crudo, FARM, Tac/Quila, The Thirsty Palms, Eight4Nine, El Patio, and Bar Cecil.

### Notable Dining Destinations:

**Upscale/Fine Dining:**
- Le Vallauris Restaurant is one of the most expensive restaurants in the U.S. and you can head to downtown Palm Springs to dine at this luxury gourmet enterprise.
- 4 Saints offers a dining experience perched on a rooftop in downtown Palm Springs that feels polished yet relaxed.

**Mexican Cuisine:**
- Clandestino serves elevated and fresh Mexican food located right next to the Marilyn Monroe statue in downtown Palm Springs.
- Tac/Quila has become a downtown favorite for its fusion of traditional Jalisco-style flavors and modern culinary creativity, with an extensive menu of Mexican spirits and tasty plates.

**Farm-to-Table:**
- Farm combines the romance of a Southwestern villa with the freshness of a working farm-to-table restaurant.

**Casual Dining:**
- Boozehounds Palm Springs is a lively American-style spot that blends casual meals with cocktails, brunch, and a welcoming energy.

Staying in downtown Palm Springs is convenient because it places visitors near popular restaurants, bars, and attractions, with many enjoying the walkability and vibrant dining scene centered around Palm Canyon Drive.

The restaurant is a tropical-chic establishment known for bold global flavors, sustainable sourcing, and a standout bar scene, marking 18 years in the valley. The Tropicale has been a local favorite since 2007 for its 1960s-inspired supper club aesthetic, eclectic menu, and tiki-style patio outfitted with palm trees, and guests can grab a spot at the 50-seat bar and start with a tropical cocktail like a mango colada before diving into Shanghai-style baby back ribs and crispy onion rings with Dijon remoulade.

Based on the search results, I found information about The Tropicale from The Infatuation's Palm Springs restaurant guide:

The Tropicale is a grown-up, kitschy oasis with an old-school Miami supper club feel. It offers a fine dining experience with an impressive cocktail list and back patio, and The Tropicale is down to party.

The Tropicale is a chic, tropical-inspired oasis that offers a lively dining experience blending bold global flavors with a sophisticated, mid-century modern vibe, known for its vibrant atmosphere, colorful cocktails, and an eclectic menu drawing on influences from Asia, Latin America, and the Mediterranean.

The restaurant is located at 330 East Amado Road, Palm Springs, CA 92262, and has been recognized as "Best of the best 2025" by OpenTable Diners Choice.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about these Palm Springs entertainment venues:

## The Purple Room Supper Club

The Purple Room is the desert's premier supper club, featuring the sophistication and glamour of the Rat Pack era. Located in the Club Trinidad Resort, it's where Frank, Sammy, Dean and their pals performed in the swinging '60s.

For live jazz specifically, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday evenings feature acclaimed jazz entertainers from 6:30 to 9:30pm. The Purple Room has been voted #1 on TripAdvisor and one of the top 100 restaurants in the country by Open Table.

The venue offers an intimate and inviting room, classic cocktails with a modern twist, world-class cuisine and the best in live entertainment - seven nights a week.

## The Tropicale

The Tropicale is one of the hippest spots in town to mingle with a star-studded crowd and embraces the Palm Springs retro vibe, with live entertainment including jazz, piano, and surprise guest singers.

Both venues capture the classic Palm Springs supper club experience with live music and dining, though the Purple Room appears to be more specifically focused on jazz entertainment while The Tropicale offers a broader entertainment mix.

content.text: I have enough data now. Notably: Infatuation DOES have a dedicated Tropicale review, which contradicts the claim of total absence from major editorial roundups. Also, Tripadvisor ranking is #26 of 309 (not #22 of 281), and language count is 6 (not 7). Let me finalize.

confirms: Yelp review count ~2,899 (confirmed via Yelp listing: '2461 Photos & 2899 Reviews')
Founders Tony Di Lembo, Ranz Weinert, Rob Kurokawa (Kurokawa surname confirmed; one source also spells 'Rob Kuenzi' but Kurokawa is more widely cited)
Tony Di Lembo remains public-facing chef/owner (Facebook handle @tonydilembo, multiple recent profiles)
Founding 2007 vs 2008 discrepancy is real — restaurant site says 2008, other sources say 2007; ~17-18 years in operation
Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best 2025' Best Happy Hour award
OpenTable '100 Hottest Restaurants in U.S.' recognition (Palm Springs Life article confirms)
Site structured data weakness: no JSON-LD/schema.org Restaurant markup, no Open Graph meta tags, address/phone/hours present only as plain HTML — independently confirmed via fetch
Multilingual site with menu sub-pages (Menus, Happy Hour, Weekly Specials visible)
Does NOT dominate generative answers for 'best Palm Springs supper club / live jazz' — Purple Room is explicitly cited as 'desert's premier supper club' and #1 on TripAdvisor for that category
Does NOT surface in top generative answers for 'best downtown Palm Springs dinner' — Front Porch, Clandestino, 4 Saints, FARM, Bar Cecil, Eight4Nine dominate
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking claim ('#22 of 281') is OUTDATED/INCORRECT — current Tripadvisor data shows #26 of 309 Palm Springs restaurants with 1,490 reviews (not 1,491, not #22/281). Rating 4.3/5 is confirmed.

Claim that Tropicale is 'notably absent from major editorial roundups (Infatuation, Bon Traveler, Time Out, Cozymeal) in generative summaries' is OVERSTATED — The Infatuation has a dedicated Tropicale review page AND includes it in their '25 Best Restaurants & Bars In Palm Springs' guide. Also surfaces in Yelp 2026 top-10 Romantic Restaurants list alongside Clandestino/FARM/Copley's/Eight4Nine/Bar Cecil, contradicting the 'does NOT surface in most romantic' claim.

Language count: site supports 6 languages (English, Italian, Spanish, French, Filipino, German, Traditional Chinese — actually 7 listed in fetch, so the claim of '7 languages' is plausible; the 6-vs-7 is a minor discrepancy worth noting)

'Most romantic Palm Springs' claim — Tropicale DOES appear in Yelp's 2026 top-10 romantic list, contradicting the claim it doesn't surface for romantic queries (though it is absent from the very-top Le Vallauris/Copley's/FARM tier in editorial generative answers)
flagged_as_unverified: Exact OpenTable review count — already flagged in original
'Palm Springs Most Booked Restaurant 2023' self-citation — search returned OpenTable's general Most Booked list including Tropicale, but no specific 2023 #1 ranking was independently confirmable
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice — not specifically surfaced in search results
Apps.thetropicale.com subdomain existence — not independently verified in this lens
Sentiment polarization (atmosphere/cocktails strong, food execution mixed) — directionally plausible but not quantitatively verified here
input.notes: Structured-data assessment is solidly confirmed — site genuinely lacks JSON-LD, Open Graph, and machine-parseable hours/menu. Credentials are mostly real. Two material errors: (1) Tripadvisor numbers are stale (#26/309, not #22/281), and (2) the editorial-absence claim is too strong — Infatuation has a dedicated Tropicale review and lists it in their Palm Springs guide, and Yelp's romantic top-10 includes it. Visibility gap score of 7 may be slightly high given Infatuation coverage; 5-6 is more defensible. Convertibility tier 'convertible' remains reasonable given the clear technical gaps and real brand equity.
